chatgpt.recommended_path: Wrap the existing React/Vite web app with Capacitor for native iOS and Android apps.
why_now: Maximizes reuse of current React codebase
Provides native access to camera and file APIs
Enables app store publishing with minimal rewrite
main_risks: Potential native plugin limitations or bugs
Performance overhead compared to pure native
App store review challenges with webview apps
must_do_before_store_release: Implement and test Capacitor plugins for camera and file upload
Ensure offline and session continuity works reliably
Optimize app startup and loading performance
Thoroughly test auth flows and WhatsApp integration
Comply with App Store and Play Store guidelines
phase_1: Integrate Capacitor into existing React/Vite app
Configure native plugins for camera and file upload
Test core flows on iOS and Android devices
Set up app store accounts and prepare metadata
phase_2: Optimize mobile UX within Capacitor shell
Add native splash screens and app icons
Conduct beta testing with real users
phase_3: Submit apps to App Store and Google Play
Monitor app performance and crash reports
Iterate on UX improvements based on feedback
Plan for future native feature enhancements
avoid: Full React Native rewrite due to high rework
Relying solely on PWA for app store presence

grok.recommended_path: Wrap existing React/Vite app with Capacitor for native iOS/Android builds.
why_now: Preserves all current code and flows
Fastest path to App Store and Play Store
main_risks: Store rejection risk for thin wrapper
must_do_before_store_release: Add app icons and splash screens
Test all document upload flows on device
Set up production signing and certificates
phase_1: Install Capacitor and add iOS/Android platforms
phase_2: Polish native navigation and status bar
Add offline handling and push config
End-to-end testing with Playwright on emulators
phase_3: Beta testing via TestFlight and Play Console

gemini.recommended_path: Wrap the existing React/Vite web app with Capacitor for native iOS and Android deployment.
why_now: Maximizes reuse of existing React/Vite codebase.
Provides reliable access to native device features.
main_risks: Potential for 'web-view feel' if not carefully styled.
Debugging across web/native boundary can be complex.
Plugin compatibility or custom plugin development for edge cases.
must_do_before_store_release: Implement Capacitor plugins for camera, file, and auth.
Configure native splash screens and app icons.
Optimize web app for Capacitor-specific performance (e.g., initial load).
Thoroughly test all key flows on target devices.
Set up push notifications (if needed) and deep linking.
phase_1: Integrate Capacitor into existing React/Vite project.
Build and run basic app on iOS/Android emulators.
Implement core Capacitor plugins (camera, file upload).
phase_2: Refine mobile UX for native feel (status bar, gestures).
Implement session continuity and simple auth via Capacitor.
Set up app icons, splash screens, and app store metadata.
Conduct extensive user acceptance testing (UAT) on devices.
phase_3: Prepare for App Store and Google Play submission.
Address any platform-specific bugs or performance issues.
Monitor app store reviews and analytics post-launch.
Plan for ongoing maintenance and updates.
avoid: Full React Native rewrite due to high cost/rework.
Pure PWA for iOS App Store and native feature reliability.
Over-customizing native code, stick to web-first approach.
